message:
fix(pass2.5): Use module-scope lookups for module-level class references

When in_unscoped_method is true (inherited methods), getZigName() skips
Pass 2.5 lookups. This was causing module-level classes to be referenced
by their Python names instead of their Pass 2.5 unique names.

Fix: Use getClassZigName() for module-level classes, which always looks
up at module scope (ID 0) regardless of in_unscoped_method flag.

Changes:
- calls.zig: Check class_registry and use getClassZigName for module classes
- generators.zig: Use getClassZigName for module-level class declarations
- comp_expr_subs.zig: Use appropriate lookup for class constructors in
  list comprehensions

Fixes errors like:
- Range undeclared (module-level class in inherited method)
- LenOnly undeclared (class declaration using wrong name)
- A undeclared (nested class in list comprehension)

Test status: test_bool 38/38, core1 14/65 (21.5%) - stable
